text.title: GraphQL Schema Patterns
text.summary: Schema design: types, enums, Relay cursor pagination, input types, error handling.

rule.statement: Use input types for all mutations. Never use positional scalar arguments for mutation inputs with more than one field.
rule.rationale: Input types are extensible without breaking clients and make tooling (IDE autocomplete, codegen) work correctly. Positional args become unmanageable with more than 2 fields.

rule.statement: Use Relay-style cursor connections for any list field that could grow. Never use offset+limit in public schema.
rule.rationale: Offset pagination is inconsistent when items are inserted/deleted mid-page. Cursor pagination is stable and composable with `hasNextPage`/`hasPreviousPage`.

rule.statement: Return structured error objects in error responses with `path`, `extensions.code`, and `message`. Never rely on HTTP status codes alone.
rule.rationale: GraphQL always returns HTTP 200. Clients parse `errors[]` in the response body. Without structured codes, error handling degenerates to string matching on messages.
